title: The Yellow Wallpaper
author: Gilman, Charlotte Perkins
summary: The narrator, recovering from 'nervous depression,' stays with her husband John at an inherited mansion. Confined to a top-floor nursery, she is forbidden work and discouraged from writing, though she secretly records her thoughts. The nursery’s ghastly yellow wallpaper captivates and irritates her; she studies its sprawling, contradictory patterns obsessively. John, a practical physician, along with the narrator’s brother, dismisses her illness as hysteria and prescribes rest, tonics, and avoidance of stimulating company. Jennie, John’s sister, manages the household. The narrator’s isolation, thwarted desire to write, and fixation on the wallpaper deepen her distress and wavering grip on self-control daily.

characters.name: Narrator
characters.personality: reflective, imaginative, increasingly anxious and obsessive
characters.lifespan: Remains confined in the house; becomes more obsessed with the wallpaper and distressed during the narrative excerpt

characters.name: John
characters.personality: practical, paternalistic, rational, a physician who dismisses emotional complaints
characters.lifespan: Acts as primary caregiver and medical authority; keeps narrator confined and prescribes rest

characters.name: Narrator's brother
characters.personality: medical, authoritative, agrees with John’s diagnosis
characters.lifespan: Supports the diagnosis of nervous depression / hysteria and endorses rest and tonics

characters.name: Jennie
characters.lifespan: Manages household tasks and cares for domestic needs; present in the house

characters.name: Mary
characters.lifespan: Looks after the baby and helps with childcare

characters.name: The baby
characters.lifespan: Present in household; cared for by Mary and Jennie

characters.name: Mother

characters.name: Nellie
characters.lifespan: Visits with mother and children for a week
